2023 FCA 42 [2023] 3 FCR 156
Federal Court of Appeal 2023
2023-06-20
Dawson J.A. Rivoalen J.A.

Facts

5

The appellant, a customs officer, discovered an importation of synthetic opioids exceeding 10kg concealed inside a shipment of legitimate pharmaceuticals.

Issues

8

Whether the seizure under s. 10 of the Customs Act was lawful given the lack of articulated reasonable grounds for the initial inspection.

Reasoning

15

The Court holds that reasonable grounds need not be established prior to the initial inspection when objective factors support a customs officer's suspicion.

Disposition

22

Appeal dismissed. The seizure is upheld as lawful under the Customs Act.
